rag_rules: Preserve owner scope: keep interface-level contract content with the component type and keep implementation-level bindings only in the implementation scope.
Do not move implementation-specific references into a type-level contract, especially paths that depend on subcomponents or connections.
Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting annex content: do not alter component structure, features, connections, or unrelated properties.
If requirement content refers to model properties rather than visible contract symbols, avoid forcing it into annex content unless the model explicitly exposes those values in scope.
generic_rules: Insert new content into the narrowest valid owner scope and avoid cross-scope leakage.
Maintain traceability by attaching each inserted block to the owning declaration without renaming unrelated model elements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Component types commonly own contract declarations, while implementations commonly own implementation-level bindings when valid in scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only references in a type-owned annex.
Do not modify architecture-defining AADL elements as part of annex insertion.
irrelevant_topics: Detailed AGREE clause syntax beyond owner/scope selection
Validator-driven type repair strategies unless diagnostics appear
